summary: Bangladesh faces mounting security and crime concerns as police recover weapons after a Narayanganj attack and a child is murdered following an attempted rape. The government is pursuing economic initiatives including major infrastructure investments like a Boeing aircraft purchase agreement and gas well drilling contracts, while grappling with inflation pressures that are driving up food prices and threatening export competitiveness. A measles-like illness claiming child lives continues to spread, adding to public health concerns. Domestically, political discourse centers on Jamaat-e-Islami's historical opposition to the Liberation War, while internationally attention focuses on escalating US-Iran tensions that threaten global commodity markets and rice supplies.
